story.soThat: the project structure and setup process are clearly explained
story.tasks: - Update README.md with project overview, prerequisites, quick start, architecture overview, contribution guidelines - Create docs/getting-started/00-prerequisites.md documenting required tools (JDK 21, Docker, Git) - Create CONTRIBUTING.md with development workflow and quality standards - Add LICENSE file (Apache 2.0) - Create .editorconfig for consistent code formatting across IDEs - Test all documentation by following instructions on clean system - Verify all documentation links are working

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. ✅ README.md updated with: project overview, prerequisites, quick start (./scripts/init-dev.sh), architecture overview, contribution guidelines 2. ✅ docs/getting-started/00-prerequisites.md documents required tools (JDK 21, Docker, Git) 3. ✅ CONTRIBUTING.md with development workflow and quality standards 4. ✅ LICENSE file (Apache 2.0 or Axians choice) 5. ✅ .editorconfig for consistent code formatting across IDEs 6. ✅ All documentation tested by following instructions on clean system 7. ✅ Documentation links verified and working

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: EAF must provide clear, comprehensive documentation enabling new developers to onboard in less than 3 days. Documentation must cover architecture overview, quick start guides, API references, and development workflows.

doc.title: Unified Decision Architecture
doc.snippet: One-command initialization (./scripts/init-dev.sh) provisions complete local development stack including PostgreSQL, Keycloak, Redis, Prometheus, and Grafana. Constitutional TDD enforced via Git hooks.

doc.title: Technical Specification - Epic 1
doc.snippet: Foundation documentation establishes the entry point for all EAF developers. Must include README.md (project overview), CONTRIBUTING.md (development workflow), .editorconfig (IDE consistency), and LICENSE file.

doc.title: Claude Code Project Instructions
doc.snippet: EAF v1.0 is a Kotlin-based enterprise framework with Hexagonal Architecture, CQRS/ES via Axon Framework, and Spring Modulith. Documentation structure includes PRD, architecture, tech-spec, epics, and 112 implementation-ready stories.

artifact.reason: Main project README already exists with overview, badges, and core features. Needs verification that all AC requirements are met.
Contributing guidelines already exist. Needs verification against Story AC requirements (TDD workflow, quality gates, PR process).
EditorConfig file already exists. Needs verification that all required settings are present (Kotlin, YAML, Markdown formatting).
LICENSE file is missing and must be created (Apache 2.0 as per AC4).
Getting started directory and prerequisites document do not exist. Must be created per AC2.
Version catalog defines all technology versions referenced in documentation (Kotlin 2.2.21, Spring Boot 3.5.7, etc.). Use these for accurate documentation.
One-command initialization script referenced in README quick start. Verify script exists and works as documented.

story-context.constraints: - All documentation must use project-relative paths (not absolute paths) - README.md must include badges for build status, coverage, and security - CONTRIBUTING.md must document Constitutional TDD requirement - .editorconfig must enforce ktlint-compatible settings (spaces, 4-indent for Kotlin) - LICENSE must be Apache 2.0 (as specified in PRD) - Documentation must be tested on clean system before marking story as done - All links in documentation must be verified as working - Getting started guide must document prerequisites for all platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows) - Documentation style: Clear, concise, developer-focused - Code examples must be tested and working

interface.name: Documentation Structure
interface.signature: docs/ ├── getting-started/ │ └── 00-prerequisites.md ├── PRD.md ├── architecture.md ├── tech-spec.md ├── epics.md └── stories/

interface.name: README.md Required Sections
interface.signature: # EAF v1.0 ## Overview ## Core Features ## Quick Start ## Prerequisites ## Architecture ## Project Structure ## Development Workflow ## Documentation ## Contributing ## License

interface.name: CONTRIBUTING.md Required Sections
interface.signature: # Contributing to EAF v1.0 ## Development Setup ## Development Workflow ## Test-Driven Development (Mandatory) ## Quality Gates ## Code Standards ## Pull Request Process ## Questions?

tests.standards:
EAF follows Constitutional TDD (Test-Driven Development) with 7-layer testing defense: 1. Static Analysis (ktlint, Detekt, Konsist) 2. Unit Tests (Kotest with Nullable Pattern) 3. Integration Tests (Testcontainers with real dependencies) 4. Property-Based Tests (Kotest property testing) 5. Fuzz Testing (Jazzer for security) 6. Concurrency Tests (LitmusKt for race conditions) 7. Mutation Testing (Pitest for test effectiveness)
Documentation testing requirements: - All code examples must be tested and working - All commands must be verified (./scripts/init-dev.sh, gradle commands) - All links must be validated (no broken links) - Documentation must be tested on clean system (fresh clone) - Screenshots and diagrams must match current implementation
tests.locations: - Documentation files: README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/getting-started/ - Test validation: Manual testing on clean system - Link validation: CI/CD broken link checker (future) - Command verification: Test all commands in documentation

test.name: Verify README.md completeness
test.approach: Manual review checklist: project overview ✓, prerequisites ✓, quick start with ./scripts/init-dev.sh ✓, architecture overview ✓, contribution guidelines link ✓

test.name: Verify prerequisites guide created
test.approach: Check docs/getting-started/00-prerequisites.md exists and documents JDK 21, Docker, Git with platform-specific instructions

test.name: Verify CONTRIBUTING.md completeness
test.approach: Manual review: TDD workflow documented ✓, quality gates explained ✓, PR process clear ✓

test.name: Verify LICENSE file exists
test.approach: Check LICENSE file exists at project root and contains Apache 2.0 license text

test.name: Verify .editorconfig completeness
test.approach: Check .editorconfig exists and includes Kotlin (4 spaces), YAML (2 spaces), Markdown settings

test.name: Test documentation on clean system
test.approach: Fresh clone → Follow README quick start → Verify all commands work → Document any issues found

test.name: Verify all documentation links
test.approach: Manually check all links in README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/getting-started/ → All links resolve correctly
